content.text:
Respond to the question:
Please share the most difficult rewriting task you faced in the FLIP game: What was the original sentence? How did you make it clearer and more specific?
回答問題：
請分享在換言一新遊戲中你覺得最困難的改寫：原句是什麼？你是如何讓它變得更清楚、具體的？
content.assessment_standard:
10	The most difficult rewriting task was changing the negative sentence 'You are always so lazy' into something positive. It was hard because the original word was very judgmental. I made it clearer and more specific by changing it to 'You really value your rest and know how to conserve your energy.' This highlighted a positive aspect of taking breaks.|I struggled to reframe the phrase 'This idea is completely stupid.' It felt too aggressive to fix easily. I transformed it into 'This idea is very unique and challenges traditional thinking.' By replacing the insult with words about creativity, I made the sentence constructive and specific rather than just negative.|Rewriting 'You talk too much' was very challenging. I wanted to keep the core meaning without the criticism. I changed it to 'You are very enthusiastic about sharing your thoughts and communicating with others.' This made the description much more appreciative and focused on their positive communication traits.
9	The most difficult rewriting task was changing 'You are always so lazy' into something positive. It was hard because the word was judgmental. I made it clearer and more specific by changing it to 'You really value your rest and know how to conserve energy.' This highlighted a positive aspect.|I struggled to reframe the phrase 'This idea is completely stupid.' It felt too aggressive. I transformed it into 'This idea is very unique and challenges traditional thinking.' By replacing the insult with creativity, I made the sentence constructive rather than negative.|Rewriting 'You talk too much' was challenging. I wanted to keep the meaning without criticism. I changed it to 'You are enthusiastic about sharing your thoughts and communicating.' This made the description appreciative and focused on positive traits.
8	The most difficult rewriting task is changing 'You are always so lazy' to positive. It is hard because word is judgmental. I make it clear and specific by change to 'You value rest and know to conserve energy.' This highlight positive aspect.|I struggle to reframe 'This idea is completely stupid.' It feel too aggressive. I transform it to 'This idea is unique and challenge traditional thinking.' By replace insult with creativity, I make sentence constructive not negative.|Rewriting 'You talk too much' is challenging. I want keep meaning without criticism. I change it to 'You are enthusiastic about share thoughts and communicate.' This make description appreciative and focus positive traits.
7	Difficult rewrite task is change 'You always lazy' to positive. Hard because word judgmental. I make clear change to 'You value rest and conserve energy.' It highlight positive.|Struggle reframe 'This idea completely stupid.' Feel aggressive. I change to 'Idea is unique and challenge tradition thinking.' Replace insult with creativity make constructive.|Rewrite 'You talk too much' is challenge. Want keep meaning no criticism. Change to 'You enthusiastic share thought and communicate.' Make appreciative focus positive.
6	Difficult rewrite change 'You always lazy' positive. Hard word judgmental. Make clear change 'You value rest conserve energy.' Highlight positive.|Struggle reframe 'Idea completely stupid.' Aggressive. Change 'Idea unique challenge tradition thinking.' Replace insult creativity constructive.|Rewrite 'You talk too much' challenge. Keep meaning no criticism. Change 'You enthusiastic share thought communicate.' Appreciative focus positive.
5	Difficult rewrite 'You always lazy' positive. Judgmental. Change 'You value rest conserve energy.' Highlight positive.|Struggle 'Idea completely stupid.' Aggressive. Change 'Idea unique challenge tradition.' Replace insult constructive.|Rewrite 'You talk too much.' No criticism. Change 'You enthusiastic share thought.' Appreciative positive.
4	Rewrite 'You lazy' positive. Change 'Value rest'. Positive.|Struggle 'Idea stupid'. Change 'Idea unique'. Constructive.|Rewrite 'Talk much'. Change 'Enthusiastic share'. Positive.
3	Rewrite lazy positive. Value rest.|Idea stupid. Idea unique.|Talk much. Enthusiastic share.
2	Rewrite lazy.|Idea stupid.|Talk much.
1	(No response / Silence)

content.text:
Respond to the question:
Please share a recent experience where you needed to reorganize or explain a piece of vague information. How did you do it?
回答問題：
請分享最近一次你需要重新整理或解釋一段模糊資訊的經驗，你是怎麼做的？
content.assessment_standard:
10	Recently, my teacher gave us a very vague instruction for an essay, saying 'just write about society.' I needed to clarify this for my study group. I reorganized the information by breaking 'society' down into specific topics like technology, education, and environment. I then suggested we each choose one concrete topic, which made the task much clearer for everyone.|I had to explain a complicated and poorly written recipe to my younger brother. The instructions just said 'mix everything until good.' I reorganized it by writing down a clear, step-by-step list with specific measurements and describing exactly what the texture should look like. This made the vague information actionable and easy to follow.|A friend texted me 'I feel bad' without any context. To help them, I asked a series of specific questions to reorganize their vague feeling. I asked if they were physically sick, stressed about school, or upset with someone. By guiding them to be specific, I helped them explain their actual problem so I could comfort them.
9	Recently, my teacher gave vague instruction for an essay, saying 'just write about society.' I needed to clarify this for my group. I reorganized the information by breaking 'society' down into specific topics like technology and education. I suggested we choose one concrete topic, making the task clearer.|I had to explain a poorly written recipe to my younger brother. The instructions said 'mix everything until good.' I reorganized it by writing a clear, step-by-step list with specific measurements and describing the texture. This made the vague information easy to follow.|A friend texted me 'I feel bad' without context. To help, I asked specific questions to reorganize their vague feeling. I asked if they were sick or stressed. By guiding them to be specific, I helped them explain their problem so I could comfort them.
8	Recently, teacher give vague instruction for essay, say 'write about society.' I clarify this for group. I reorganize information by break 'society' to specific topics like technology and education. I suggest we choose one concrete topic, make task clear.|I have to explain poorly written recipe to brother. Instruction say 'mix everything good.' I reorganize it by write clear step list with measurements and describe texture. This make vague information easy to follow.|Friend text me 'I feel bad' without context. To help, I ask specific questions to reorganize vague feeling. I ask if sick or stress. By guide them specific, I help explain problem and comfort them.
7	Teacher give vague instruction essay 'write about society.' I clarify for group. Reorganize info by break 'society' to specific topic technology education. Suggest choose one concrete topic make clear.|Explain poor recipe to brother. Say 'mix everything good.' Reorganize write clear step list with measure describe texture. Make vague info easy follow.|Friend text 'I feel bad' no context. Help ask specific question reorganize vague feeling. Ask if sick stress. Guide specific help explain problem comfort.
6	Teacher vague instruction essay 'write society.' Clarify group. Reorganize info break 'society' specific topic technology. Suggest choose concrete topic make clear.|Explain poor recipe brother. Say 'mix good.' Reorganize write clear step list measure describe texture. Make vague info easy.|Friend text 'I feel bad' no context. Ask specific question reorganize vague feeling. Ask sick stress. Guide specific explain problem.
5	Teacher vague instruction essay. Clarify group. Reorganize info break specific topic. Make clear.|Explain poor recipe brother. Reorganize write clear step list measure. Make vague easy.|Friend text 'feel bad'. Ask specific question reorganize vague. Guide specific explain.
4	Teacher vague essay. Reorganize specific topic. Clear.|Explain poor recipe. Write clear step. Easy.|Friend feel bad. Ask specific question. Explain.
3	Vague essay. Specific topic.|Poor recipe. Clear step.|Feel bad. Specific question.
2	Vague essay.|Poor recipe.|Feel bad.
1	(No response / Silence)

content.text:
Respond to the question:
Which part do you feel you need to improve the most when communicating? (e.g., too abstract, speaking too fast, too much information, unclear focus) Why?
回答問題：
你覺得自己在溝通時最需要改進哪一部分？(例如：太抽象、講太快、資訊太多、重點不明) 為什麼？
content.assessment_standard:
10	I feel I most need to improve being less abstract when communicating. I often use big concepts and assume people understand exactly what I mean, which leads to confusion. If I practice giving concrete examples and specific details to support my points, my communication will become much clearer and more effective.|My biggest issue is that I include too much information at once. When I explain something, I tend to go off on tangents and lose the main point. I need to improve by structuring my thoughts beforehand and focusing only on the most relevant details to keep my audience engaged and understanding the core message.|I definitely need to improve my speaking speed. When I get excited or nervous, I talk incredibly fast, making it hard for others to catch my words. Taking conscious pauses, breathing regularly, and slowing down my pace will greatly enhance my ability to be understood by others.
9	I feel I need to improve being less abstract when communicating. I often use big concepts and assume people understand, which leads to confusion. If I practice giving concrete examples to support my points, my communication will become clearer and more effective.|My biggest issue is including too much information at once. When I explain something, I go off on tangents and lose the main point. I need to improve by structuring my thoughts beforehand and focusing on the most relevant details.|I need to improve my speaking speed. When I get excited or nervous, I talk incredibly fast, making it hard for others to catch my words. Taking pauses, breathing, and slowing down will enhance my ability to be understood.
8	I feel I need to improve less abstract when communicate. I use big concepts and assume people understand, it lead to confusion. If I practice give concrete examples support points, my communication become clear and effective.|My biggest issue is include too much information at once. When explain something, I go off tangents and lose main point. I need improve structure thoughts and focus on relevant details.|I need improve speaking speed. When excited or nervous, I talk very fast, make hard for others catch words. Take pause, breathe, and slow down enhance ability to be understood.
7	I need improve less abstract when communicate. I use big concept and think people understand, it make confusion. If I give concrete example, communication become clear.|My issue is too much information at once. When explain, I lose main point. I need improve structure thought and focus relevant detail.|I need improve speak speed. When nervous, I talk fast, hard for other catch word. Take pause and slow down make me understand better.
6	Need improve less abstract communicate. Use big concept think people understand make confusion. Give concrete example communication clear.|Issue too much information. When explain lose main point. Improve structure thought focus relevant detail.|Need improve speak speed. Nervous talk fast hard other catch word. Take pause slow down understand better.
5	Improve less abstract communicate. Big concept confusion. Give concrete example clear.|Issue too much information. Explain lose main point. Structure thought focus detail.|Improve speak speed. Nervous talk fast. Take pause slow down.
4	Improve abstract communicate. Big concept confusion. Concrete example.|Too much info. Lose main point. Structure thought.|Improve speak speed. Nervous talk fast. Slow down.
3	Abstract communicate. Confusion.|Too much info. Lose point.|Speak speed. Talk fast.
2	Abstract.|Too much info.|Speak speed.
1	(No response / Silence)

content.text:
Respond to the question:
How did you feel when another player successfully understood the positive meaning behind your reframed negative sentence?
回答問題：
當其他玩家成功理解你將負面句子改寫後的正面意義時，你有什麼感受？
content.assessment_standard:
10	When another player successfully understood the positive meaning behind my reframed sentence, I felt a deep sense of accomplishment and connection. It was incredibly rewarding to know that my careful choice of words effectively changed a negative concept into a shared positive understanding. It made me realize the true power of empathetic communication.|I felt very relieved and happy when they guessed my meaning. I was worried my rewriting was too subtle or confusing. Their success validated my effort to find the right words, and it created a really warm, supportive atmosphere around the table. It boosted my confidence in my communication skills.|Seeing their eyes light up with understanding gave me a huge thrill. It felt like we had decoded a secret language together. It proved to me that even the most negative thoughts can be transformed into something beautiful and understandable if we just take the time to choose our words wisely.
9	When another player understood the positive meaning behind my reframed sentence, I felt a deep sense of accomplishment. It was rewarding to know my careful choice of words changed a negative concept into a positive understanding. It made me realize the power of empathetic communication.|I felt very relieved and happy when they guessed my meaning. I was worried my rewriting was too confusing. Their success validated my effort to find the right words, and created a warm, supportive atmosphere. It boosted my confidence.|Seeing their eyes light up with understanding gave me a huge thrill. It felt like we decoded a secret language together. It proved that negative thoughts can be transformed into something beautiful if we choose words wisely.
8	When player understand positive meaning behind reframed sentence, I feel deep sense of accomplishment. It is rewarding to know my choice of words change negative concept to positive understanding. It make me realize power of good communication.|I feel relieved and happy when they guess my meaning. I worry my rewrite is confusing. Their success validate my effort to find right words, and create warm atmosphere. It boost my confidence.|See their eyes light up with understanding give me huge thrill. It feel like we decode secret language together. It prove negative thoughts can transform to beautiful if we choose words wise.
7	When player understand positive meaning reframed sentence, I feel sense accomplishment. Rewarding know my words change negative to positive understanding. Make me realize power communication.|I feel relieve and happy when guess meaning. I worry rewrite confusing. Success validate effort find right words, create warm atmosphere. Boost confidence.|See eyes light up understanding give thrill. Feel like decode secret language together. Prove negative thought transform beautiful if choose word wise.
6	Player understand positive meaning reframed sentence, I feel accomplishment. Rewarding know words change negative positive understanding. Realize power communication.|Feel relieve happy guess meaning. Worry rewrite confusing. Success validate effort find right words, create warm atmosphere. Boost confidence.|See eyes light up understanding give thrill. Feel decode secret language together. Prove negative thought transform beautiful choose word wise.
5	Understand positive meaning reframed. Feel accomplishment. Words change negative positive. Power communication.|Relieve happy guess meaning. Validate effort right words. Warm atmosphere boost confidence.|Eyes light up understanding thrill. Decode secret language. Negative transform beautiful.
4	Understand positive meaning. Feel accomplishment. Words change.|Relieve happy guess. Right words. Confidence.|Eyes light up. Secret language. Transform beautiful.
3	Understand positive. Accomplishment.|Relieve happy. Confidence.|Eyes light. Transform.
2	Understand positive.|Relieve happy.|Eyes light.
1	(No response / Silence)

content.text:
Respond to the question:
Can you describe a moment when changing the words you used completely changed your own mood or perspective on a problem?
回答問題：
你能描述一次改變用詞後，完全改變了你自己的心情或對問題看法的經驗嗎？
content.assessment_standard:
10	I remember being very stressed about a 'failed' exam. I kept telling myself 'I am a failure.' But when I consciously changed my words to 'I discovered areas where I need to study more,' my mood instantly lifted. This simple shift in vocabulary changed my perspective from a state of defeat to a motivated, growth-oriented mindset.|Once, I was angry because my friend was 'wasting my time' by being late. I felt resentful. But I decided to reframe it in my mind as 'I have fifteen minutes of unexpected free time to relax.' That change in words completely erased my anger and actually made me enjoy the wait.|I used to describe my daily chores as 'burdens I have to do.' This made me feel exhausted before even starting. When I started calling them 'acts of caring for my living space,' my mood shifted completely. The new words made the tasks feel meaningful rather than oppressive, showing how language dictates emotion.
9	I remember being stressed about a 'failed' exam. I kept telling myself 'I am a failure.' When I consciously changed my words to 'I discovered areas where I need to study more,' my mood instantly lifted. This shift changed my perspective from defeat to a motivated mindset.|Once, I was angry because my friend was 'wasting my time' by being late. I decided to reframe it as 'I have fifteen minutes of unexpected free time to relax.' That change in words completely erased my anger and made me enjoy the wait.|I described my chores as 'burdens I have to do.' This made me feel exhausted. When I started calling them 'acts of caring for my living space,' my mood shifted. The new words made the tasks feel meaningful rather than oppressive.
8	I remember being stress about 'failed' exam. I tell myself 'I am failure.' When I change words to 'I discover areas I need study more,' my mood lift. This shift change perspective from defeat to motivated mindset.|Once I angry because friend 'waste my time' being late. I decide reframe as 'I have unexpected free time relax.' That change in words erase anger and make me enjoy wait.|I describe chores as 'burdens I have to do.' Make me exhausted. When I call them 'acts caring for living space,' my mood shift. New words make tasks feel meaningful not oppressive.
7	I stress about 'fail' exam. Tell myself 'I failure.' Change words to 'I discover area need study,' mood lift. Shift change perspective from defeat to motivated.|Once angry friend 'waste time' late. Decide reframe 'I have free time relax.' Change words erase anger make enjoy wait.|Describe chores 'burdens have to do.' Exhausted. Call them 'caring for living space,' mood shift. New words make task meaningful.
6	Stress 'fail' exam. Tell 'I failure.' Change words 'discover area need study,' mood lift. Shift change perspective defeat motivated.|Angry friend 'waste time' late. Reframe 'have free time relax.' Change words erase anger enjoy wait.|Describe chores 'burdens.' Exhausted. Call 'caring living space,' mood shift. New words meaningful.
5	Stress fail exam. Tell failure. Change words discover need study mood lift. Perspective motivated.|Angry friend waste time. Reframe free time relax. Change words erase anger.|Describe chores burdens. Call caring living space mood shift. Words meaningful.
4	Stress fail exam. Change words study. Motivated.|Angry friend waste time. Free time relax. Erase anger.|Chores burdens. Caring space. Mood shift.
3	Fail exam. Change words.|Waste time. Free time.|Chores burdens. Caring.
2	Change words.|Free time.|Mood shift.
1	(No response / Silence)
